description: Public-safe production cutover checklist for moving 6529bot from reviewed source to controlled live dogfood traffic.

phases.title: Release Baseline
phases.objective: Start from a reviewed commit, clean release checks, and private operator evidence before creating or changing live infrastructure.

items.title: Cutover source commit or tag is reviewed, merged, and recorded in private operator evidence.
items.evidence: git rev-parse HEAD plus the release PR or tag decision

items.title: npm run release:check passes from a clean checkout.
items.evidence: release check output captured in private operator evidence

items.title: GitHub CI, Dependency Review, and OpenSSF Scorecard for the cutover commit are reviewed.
items.evidence: public check run links or public-safe check summaries

items.title: Release candidate bundle is rendered from current private release-gate status and operator evidence.
items.evidence: npm run release:candidate with private status and evidence files

phases.title: GitHub App Registration
phases.objective: Create or update the production 6529bot GitHub App without copying generated credentials into public artifacts.

items.title: GitHub App manifest is rendered for the production host and reviewed before registration.
items.evidence: npm run github-app:manifest -- -- --host <production-bot-origin> --quiet

items.title: Production GitHub App named 6529bot is created or verified with documented permissions and events.
items.evidence: public-safe registration packet summary

items.title: Generated App id, private key, client secret, and webhook secret are stored only in the bot runtime secret store.
items.evidence: secret presence evidence, not secret values

items.title: GitHub App installation scope starts with selected trusted repositories for dogfood.
items.evidence: selected-repository installation summary without private repository details

phases.title: AWS Ledger And Secrets
phases.objective: Prepare isolated spend, job, run-control, price, budget, and alert infrastructure before model calls.

items.title: AWS IAM/OIDC trust and identity policies are reviewed from least-privilege templates or equivalent private policy artifacts.
items.evidence: policy review summary without live account ids or ARNs

items.title: Aurora ledger schema is applied and re-apply safe in the production ledger.
items.evidence: npm run ledger:schema -- -- --apply plus base table and managed view counts

items.title: Central budget policies are reviewed and applied, or the release explicitly keeps conservative environment caps.
items.evidence: npm run budget-policies dry-run and apply summaries

items.title: Model price rows are source-reviewed, fresh under policy, and applied, or conservative default estimates are accepted.
items.evidence: npm run model-prices dry-run and apply summaries

items.title: Provider, GitHub App, AWS, alert, worker-dispatch, and admin-auth secrets are present only in bot-owned server-side stores.
items.evidence: secret presence summary, not secret values

phases.title: Server And Worker
phases.objective: Deploy the central App server and enable worker traffic in deliberately small steps.

items.title: Container publish plan is reviewed for an operator-owned registry before any image build or push, then the image is built from the reviewed commit, scanned, and recorded by digest.
items.evidence: npm run container:publish-plan -- -- --image <operator-registry>/6529reviewbot --release v0.1.0 --require-ready plus image digest, builder identity, source commit, and scan summary kept in private evidence

items.title: Central App server is deployed first with REVIEWBOT_WORKER_ADAPTER=noop.
items.evidence: deployment summary and /healthz result

items.title: npm run preflight -- -- --strict passes in the release-candidate environment, or every warning is accepted in release notes.
items.evidence: strict preflight output summary

items.title: Invalid webhook signatures fail, GitHub App ping succeeds, and a saved pull_request delivery replays in dry-run mode.
items.evidence: public-safe webhook acceptance summary

items.title: Worker dispatch credential posture is reviewed before enabling non-noop worker traffic, with a dispatch-only GitHub App preferred and any main-App or token fallback explicitly accepted.
items.evidence: dispatch mode, dispatch-only App installation or reviewed fallback acceptance, Actions: write scope, and strict preflight warning decision kept in private evidence

items.title: Worker path is enabled only after noop verification, with conservative capacity and command-only dogfood.
items.evidence: worker adapter, capacity, and command-only config summary

items.title: Run-control claims are enforced with conservative caps, or enforcement is explicitly deferred for dogfood-only release notes.
items.evidence: run-control mode, ledger, and cap summary

phases.title: 6529.io And Alerts
phases.objective: Wire human visibility through 6529.io and scheduled operator alerts without browser access to bot secrets or Aurora.

items.title: Dashboard deployment plan is rendered with explicit 6529.io origin, bot origin, private workspace, and auth-check URL before public or private dashboard exposure.
items.evidence: npm run dashboard:deployment-plan -- -- --frontend-origin <6529-io-origin> --bot-origin <production-bot-origin> --operator-workspace <private-workspace-dir> --auth-check-url <6529-auth-check-url> --require-ready

items.title: 6529.io public transparency route is merged, deployed, wired to the public usage summary API, and limited to reviewed public repo/org disclosure allowlists, or explicitly deferred.
items.evidence: route, API target, reviewed REVIEWBOT_USAGE_API_PUBLIC_ORGS or repo allowlist decision, and deployment summary

items.title: 6529.io private admin surface calls bot-owned admin APIs through server-side HMAC signing.
items.evidence: admin route and auth-bridge summary without HMAC secrets

items.title: Private admin snapshot command is run and reviewed before exposing admin dashboards to operators.
items.evidence: npm run admin:snapshot -- -- --base-url <production-bot-origin> --require-ok

items.title: Alert delivery plan is rendered with explicit production bot origin, private workspace, webhook/SNS/SES notify mode, and operator channel label before scheduled alert delivery.
items.evidence: npm run alerts:delivery-plan -- -- --bot-origin <production-bot-origin> --operator-workspace <private-workspace-dir> --notify-mode <webhook|sns|ses> --alert-channel <operator-alert-channel> --require-ready

items.title: Scheduled spend, failed-job, and stale-claim alerts deliver to an operator-owned private channel after alert delivery plan review.
items.evidence: alert dry-run and delivery summary without webhook URLs, SNS ARNs, SES addresses, or raw payloads

phases.title: Dogfood
phases.objective: Prove command-only and limited initial review behavior on trusted repositories before broad community use.

items.title: Dogfood target repository config is merged from reviewed base-ref content and validates locally.
items.evidence: npm run validate:repo-config for the dogfood repository config

items.title: Dogfood promotion packet passes with target config, self-dogfood replay, operator workspace, reviewed model price coverage, and preflight gates included.
items.evidence: npm --silent run dogfood:promotion -- -- --operator-workspace <private-workspace-dir> --model-price-file <reviewed-model-price-file.json> --strict-preflight --require-ready

items.title: Maintainer-triggered command-only review runs with budget, job, usage, and run-control evidence.
items.evidence: public-safe bot comment and private ledger summaries

items.title: Limited initial PR review is enabled only after command-only dogfood is healthy.
items.evidence: initial-review run summary, cost summary, and rollback verification

phases.title: Rollback And Decision
phases.objective: Confirm operators can stop spend quickly and make a clear tag/no-tag decision.

items.title: Rollback controls are tested: noop worker, disabled public repo mode, disabled repo config, App uninstall, and provider-key disablement.
items.evidence: rollback exercise summary without live identifiers

items.title: Manual security review checklist is completed for the cutover commit.
items.evidence: security-review checklist summary

items.title: Operator release decision is recorded as approve, approve with follow-up, defer, or block.
items.evidence: release decision entry in private operator evidence and public-safe release notes
